home.quote:
There is much art in this world –
body control is one form of it
home.disciplinesDescription: Mastering balance across multiple terrains. Each sport brings a unique perspective on movement and flow.

videos.pageDescription: Watch hosted action-sports videos with dedicated playback pages for better search indexation.
videos.notFoundText: The requested video page does not exist. Explore the video library below.

handstands.intro: I love pushing my handstand practice into unconventional, beautiful landscapes. The challenge of finding stability on new terrain is what makes every moment both humbling and exhilarating.
handstands.noImages: No images in this selection yet.

skiing.tagline: carving peaks and painting snow in motion
skiing.p1: What I love most about skiing is the precision it demands. There's nothing like the moment when you find your line down a steep pitch—when the snow, your edges, and your body all work as one. The technical challenge excites me. Every variable—the snow condition, the slope angle, the weather—presents a new puzzle to solve.
skiing.p2: But it's not just about technique. It's about the mountains themselves. Standing at 11,000 feet with nothing but slopes and sky around you creates a sense of freedom I can't find anywhere else. Each run is a conversation with the mountain, where I'm constantly learning, adapting, pushing my limits just a little further.
skiing.equipmentText: Alpine skiing requires a precise setup: well-fitted ski boots that transfer every micro-movement to your edges, skis matched to your style and the terrain, adjustable poles for balance and rhythm, a certified helmet, UV-protective goggles, and layered technical clothing that breathes while insulating. Every piece of gear serves a purpose—there’s no room for compromise at speed.

snowboarding.tagline: rhythmic flow—where the mountain becomes your canvas
snowboarding.p1: I fell in love with snowboarding the moment I realized it's not about speed—it's about style. There's an artistry to a perfectly executed carve that you can feel through your entire body. The way the edge bites into the snow, the subtle weight shifts, the flow from one turn into the next—it's like dancing with gravity.
snowboarding.p2: What draws me back season after season is how dynamic it is. You could ride the same run a hundred times and have a hundred different experiences depending on the snow, the conditions, and your own state of mind. That constant possibility, that endless variation—that's what I'm chasing.
snowboarding.equipmentText: A solid snowboard setup starts with the right board—camber profile, flex, and width matched to your riding style. Responsive bindings connect your intentions to the board, while properly fitted boots provide the comfort and control for all-day sessions. Add a certified helmet, anti-fog goggles, and layered outerwear that handles everything from powder days to spring slush.

freediving.tagline: listening to the depth—one breath, infinite presence
freediving.p1: When I'm underwater with only a single breath, everything else disappears. No phone, no worries, no noise—just me, the silence, and the infinite blue. That moment when I let go of conscious breathing and enter a state of complete calm is what I live for. It's pure meditation.
freediving.p2: I love freediving because it forces you to be present in a way few things can. You can't overthink when your oxygen is limited. You can't be distracted. Your mind becomes a calm, steady instrument focused on one thing: the present moment. And in that presence, something magical happens—you become truly connected to the ocean.
freediving.p3: There's also a challenge that excites me. Pushing your depth safely, understanding your body's response, training your mind to stay relaxed under stress. Every dive I push a little deeper, stay a little longer, find a little more control. It's exploration in the purest sense—discovering what you're capable of when you trust yourself and respect the ocean.
freediving.p4: What keeps calling me back to the water is simple: the feeling that I'm part of something larger than myself. Down there, in that blue world, I feel more alive than anywhere else on Earth.
freediving.certDesc: Certified to dive to depths of 20+ meters on a single breath. Trained in rescue protocols, equalization techniques, and breath-hold physiology.
freediving.equipmentText: Freediving demands equipment designed for minimal drag and maximum efficiency. A low-volume mask reduces equalization effort, while long bi-fins or a monofin convert leg power into smooth propulsion. A well-fitted wetsuit maintains warmth without restricting the diaphragm, and a rubber weight belt fine-tunes buoyancy. A freediving computer tracks depth and time, and a safety lanyard keeps you connected to the line. Every gram matters when you’re working on a single breath.

highlining.tagline: absolute focus—where mind becomes one with fiber
highlining.p1: Highlining is where I've found the deepest sense of calm under extreme conditions. Walking an inch-wide line suspended hundreds of meters above the ground forces you to surrender all control except for what's in your mind. There's no room for distraction or doubt.
highlining.p2: What I love about it is the clarity it brings. When you're up there, with nothing but the webbing beneath your feet and the air all around you, your mind becomes perfectly still. All the noise of everyday life disappears. You're completely present, completely focused, completely alive.
highlining.equipmentText: Highlining requires specialized, rigorously inspected equipment. The webbing itself—typically one-inch polyester or nylon—is tensioned between bombproof anchors with ratchets or pulleys. A climbing harness and leash keep you attached at all times, connected via certified carabiners. Tree protectors and backup lines add redundancy. Every piece is load-rated and regularly retired—there is zero margin for equipment failure at height.

iceskating.tagline: floating on razor's edge—where physics becomes poetry
iceskating.p1: Ice skating calls to me because it's where physics meets feeling. There's no friction, no second chances—just pure edge awareness and the constant micro-decisions your body makes.
iceskating.p2: I love the instant feedback: carve deeper, you go faster; lean wrong, you feel it immediately. Every session is like having a conversation with the ice.
iceskating.equipmentText: Quality ice skates are everything—stiff boots that transfer every ankle movement to the blade, with edges sharpened to a precise hollow that matches your style. Blade guards protect your edges off the ice, while moisture-wicking layers and fitted gloves keep you warm without restricting movement. A well-maintained pair of skates becomes an extension of your feet.

inlineskating.tagline: cruising on the streets, seamlessly blending into traffic
inlineskating.p1: I fell in love with inline skating on city streets. There's a unique freedom in exploring urban space on eight wheels—finding lines through architecture that most people never notice. It's detective work mixed with pure flow. You're reading the city like a map of possibilities.
inlineskating.p2: What keeps me coming back is the speed combined with technical precision. You're constantly making micro-adjustments, feeling the pavement through your boots, responding to every texture change. And there's a meditative quality to it—rolling through the city at night with nothing but the sound of wheels and the rush of air.
inlineskating.equipmentText: Urban inline skating centers on a hard-shell boot with a responsive frame—typically 4×80mm wheels for the perfect balance of speed and maneuverability. A certified helmet and wrist guards are non-negotiable for street sessions, with knee and elbow pads for aggressive riding. Bearings and wheel hardness can be tuned to your surface and style. The right setup disappears beneath you, letting the city become your only focus.

mountaineering.tagline: vertical discipline—conquering oneself before the summit
mountaineering.p1: Mountaineering calls to me because it's the purest form of exploration. When you're at 19,000 feet with nothing but rock, ice, and sky around you, you find out who you really are. The mountain doesn't care about your ego or your goals—it demands respect, focus, and complete presence.
mountaineering.p2: What I love is the challenge on every level. Physically, you're pushing your body to its limits in an unforgiving environment. Mentally, you're problem-solving constantly—finding the route, managing risk, staying composed. And spiritually, there's something profound about being in places so few humans ever reach, witnessing landscapes that few eyes have ever seen.
mountaineering.equipmentText: Mountaineering demands equipment you can trust with your life. Rigid boots anchor crampons on ice and mixed terrain, while an ice axe provides self-arrest capability on steep slopes. A certified harness, dynamic rope, and protection hardware handle technical sections. Add a helmet for rockfall, layered expedition clothing rated to extreme cold, and a reliable navigation system. Every item must perform flawlessly in the most demanding conditions on earth.

pumpfoiling.tagline: weightless motion—flying without wind, conquering without engines
pumpfoiling.p1: Pumpfoiling is my closest experience to actual flight. There's something magical about rising above the water without a motor, without the wind—just pure human power converting into weightless, effortless glide. When you get the rhythm right, when your legs sync with the foil's movement, you enter a flow state that's unlike anything else.
pumpfoiling.p2: I love the challenge of it. Every session is a puzzle—finding the right technique, understanding how the foil responds, pushing your fitness and efficiency. And that moment when you stop sinking and start flying? That's what keeps me coming back. It's the result of perfect timing, perfect power, perfect presence.
pumpfoiling.equipmentText: Pumpfoiling revolves around the hydrofoil—a mast, front wing, stabilizer, and fuselage that together generate lift from water. The board is compact and light, designed to transfer pumping energy efficiently. Wing size determines the balance between lift and speed: larger wings for easier takeoff, smaller ones for high-speed efficiency. A wetsuit and impact vest provide warmth and protection. Small adjustments to mast position or shim angle change everything.

flowarts.tagline: rhythmic meditation—objects become extensions of intent
flowarts.p1: Flowarts are my creative meditation. There's something about spinning props in rhythm—whether it's poi, juggling balls, or a rope—that puts me in a headspace I can't find anywhere else. It's pure flow state: your mind quiets down, your body takes over, and you become one with the movement.
flowarts.p2: What I love is that there's no end goal. You're not competing or trying to reach a destination. You're just exploring what's possible with rhythm, timing, and expression. It's art disguised as sport, and that's exactly why I'm drawn to it.
flowarts.equipmentText: Flowarts gear is as varied as the discipline itself. Poi—whether sock poi for beginners or LED poi for performances—are the most common entry point. Contact juggling balls, staffs, and rope darts each open different movement vocabularies. For fire performances, Kevlar wicks and fuel safety equipment are essential. The key is starting simple and letting the props evolve with your practice.

surfskating.tagline: flowing lines where pavement answers back
surfskating.p1: Surfskating gave me the sensation I had been looking for all along: the rhythm of surfing translated onto dry land. What pulls me in is the feeling of linking turns with intention, reading slight slopes, and letting the board move in long, clean arcs instead of forcing speed.
surfskating.p2: Every session becomes a study in timing and style. The pavement turns into a wave substitute, and small details start to matter: body position, shoulder rotation, how smoothly one carve dissolves into the next. That simplicity is exactly why it stays so compelling.
surfskating.equipmentText: The surfskate setup is all about the truck system. A specialized front truck adapter—like Carver’s CX or C7, Yow’s Meraki, or Smoothstar’s Thruster—provides the surf-like turning radius that defines the sport. Paired with a mid-length deck and grippy soft wheels, it translates weight shifts into fluid arcs. A helmet is essential for bowl and ramp sessions, and slide gloves expand the trick vocabulary.

viaferrata.tagline: iron pathways—where ancient rock meets human spirit
viaferrata.p1: Via Ferrata calls to me because of the unique relationship it creates between my body and vertical rock. Hundreds of meters above the ground, attached to iron rungs bolted into stone, I find a clarity that comes from pure focus. The exposure strips away everything else.
viaferrata.p2: What I love is how it democratizes verticality—you don't need elite climbing skills to experience the profound presence of being on a sheer cliff face. Every rung is a decision, every meter is a meditation. That's where I feel most alive.
viaferrata.equipmentText: Via Ferrata requires a certified climbing helmet, a sit harness, and a dedicated via ferrata lanyard set with energy absorber—the critical piece that arrests a fall on the steel cable. Sturdy approach shoes or mountaineering boots with stiff soles grip iron rungs and rock confidently. Reinforced gloves protect hands from metal abrasion. Every component is rated to withstand fall forces well beyond body weight.

weightlifting.tagline: perfect form—transforming force into grace under load
weightlifting.p1: Weightlifting fascinates me because it's the ultimate blend of power and precision. There's nothing ambiguous about it—you either lift the weight or you don't. But what I love is the craft of finding the perfect technique to make heavy weight feel light.
weightlifting.p2: Every session is me learning my body better—how to generate power from the ground up, how to position myself for maximum efficiency, how to maintain focus under extreme load. It's deeply technical, incredibly challenging, and absolutely rewarding when it all clicks.
weightlifting.equipmentText: Olympic weightlifting centers on a quality barbell—20kg for men, 15kg for women—with calibrated bumper plates that can be safely dropped from overhead. Lifting shoes with a raised heel and rigid sole provide the ankle mobility and stable base essential for deep squats. Wrist wraps support under heavy loads, a leather belt braces the core for max-effort lifts, and chalk ensures a secure grip. Simplicity is the point—every tool earns its place.

calisthenics.tagline: mastering gravity through bodyweight and intent
calisthenics.p1: What draws me to calisthenics is the intimate relationship between mind and body. There's no equipment to hide behind—just you and gravity, figuring out what your body is capable of when your mind is completely focused.
calisthenics.p2: Every static hold, every handstand, every muscle-up is a conversation with my own potential. I love how progression is so tangible—yesterday I couldn't do it, today I can. That's the magic of bodyweight training: total control over your own advancement, one rep at a time. It's like learning a physical language that only my body understands.
calisthenics.equipmentText: The beauty of calisthenics is its minimalism. A sturdy pull-up bar and a set of gymnastics rings are enough to build an entire practice. Parallettes add depth to your handstand and push work, while resistance bands assist progression on moves you’re still learning. Chalk for grip, comfortable training wear, and a flat surface—that’s all you need to master your own bodyweight.

services_page.heading: Boost
services_page.subheading: Whether you are looking to push your own limits, capture incredible action footage, or partner on an exciting project, I offer a range of professional services.
services_page.s1desc: One-on-one sessions focused on progression, technique, and overcoming mental blocks in any of the disciplines.
services_page.s2desc: Professional action sports videography and photography. Capture your best moments with high-end production.
services_page.s3desc: High-performance pumpfoiling gear available for rental. Experience the sensation of flying above the water with premium equipment.
services_page.s4desc: Detailed breakdown of your technique via video. Receive personalized feedback and actionable drills to level up your skills remotely.
services_page.s5desc: Professional guiding on the iconic San Salvatore Via Ferrata in Lugano. Experience one of Switzerland's most dramatic routes with expert assistance.
services_page.s6desc: Special accommodation discount code for Saas-Fee stays via Airbnb listing references.

legal.title: Legal Notice (Impressum)
legal.name: Stefan Carlen
legal.disclaimerText: The content of this website has been prepared with the utmost care. However, no guarantee can be given for the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the content. As a service provider, I am responsible for my own content on these pages in accordance with general laws. However, I am not obligated to monitor transmitted or stored third-party information, or to investigate circumstances that indicate illegal activity. Obligations to remove or block the use of information under general law remain unaffected.
legal.externalLinksText: This website contains links to third-party websites over whose content I have no control. I therefore cannot accept any liability for this external content. The respective provider or operator of the pages is always responsible for the content of the linked pages. The linked pages were checked for possible legal violations at the time of linking. Illegal content was not recognizable at the time of linking.
legal.copyrightText: The content and works created by the site operator on these pages are subject to Swiss copyright law. Reproduction, editing, distribution, and any kind of use beyond the limits of copyright law require the written consent of the respective author or creator.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.introText: Protecting your personal data is important to me. This privacy policy explains what information is collected when you visit this website and how it is used. This website is operated in compliance with the Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (nDSG) and, where applicable, the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
privacy.cookielessText: This website does not use tracking cookies, marketing cookies, or any third-party advertising scripts. No cookie consent banner is required because no cookies are set that require your consent. Your browsing experience remains uninterrupted and private.
privacy.functionalText: A single preference — your selected language (English or German) — is stored locally in your browser using localStorage. This is a purely functional setting that stays on your device and is never transmitted to any server. No personal data is collected through this mechanism.
privacy.analyticsText: This website uses cookieless, privacy-focused analytics that do not track individual users, do not use cookies, and do not collect personal data. Aggregated, anonymized usage statistics (such as page views and referrer information) may be collected to improve the website. No data is shared with third parties for advertising purposes.
privacy.fontsText: This website uses fonts provided by Google (Google Fonts). When you open a page, your browser loads the required fonts from Google's servers. This establishes a connection to Google servers and may transmit your IP address. Google's privacy policy applies to this data processing: https://policies.google.com/privacy. The use of Google Fonts ensures a consistent, visually appealing presentation of this website.
privacy.hostingText: This website is hosted by Netlify, Inc. When you visit this site, Netlify may process technical data such as your IP address, browser type, and access times in server logs. This processing is necessary for delivering the website and is covered by Netlify's privacy policy: https://www.netlify.com/privacy.
privacy.rightsText: Under the Swiss nDSG and, where applicable, the GDPR, you have the right to request information about your stored personal data, its origin, recipients, and the purpose of processing. You also have the right to request correction or deletion of this data. For any privacy-related inquiries, please contact me at the email address provided in the Legal Notice.
privacy.changesText: This privacy policy may be updated from time to time to reflect changes in legal requirements or website functionality. The current version is always available on this page.
